mission.title: opencost: No request or usage data found during CPU allocation calculation
mission.description: No request or usage data found during CPU allocation calculation. This issue affects 7+ users.

steps.title: Identify opencost analyze symptoms
steps.description:
Check for the issue in your opencost deployment:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n opencost -l app.kubernetes.io/name=opencost
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=opencost -n opencost --tail=100 | grep -i error
```
Look for errors or warnings in the logs that may indicate the issue.

steps.title: Review opencost configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant opencost configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n opencost -l app.kubernetes.io/name=opencost
kubectl get configmap -n opencost -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=opencost
```
Warning in opencost container logs, warning are repeated every couple of minutes:

```
kubectl get pods -n opencost
opencost-844c646598-8c6d7   2/2     Running   0          80m

kubectl logs opencost-844c646598-8c6d7 opencost -n

steps.title: Apply the fix for No request or usage data found during CPU allocation…
steps.description:
Hey ! Thanks for the details provided here. Those logs can be safely ignored at this time! You can reduce the log level by setting the `LOG_LEVEL=warn` environment variable. If you installed via helm, there are some details here:
```yaml
kubectl get pods -n opencost
opencost-844c646598-8c6d7   2/2     Running   0          80m

kubectl logs opencost-844c646598-8c6d7 opencost -n opencost
2023-11-02T10:32:36.413083616Z WRN No request or usage data found during CPU allocation calculation. Setting allocation to 0.
2023-11-02T10:32:36.413303319Z WRN No request or usage data found during CPU allocation calculation. Setting allocation to 0.
2023-11-02T10:32:36.41340512Z WRN No request or usage data found during CPU allocation calculation. Setting allocation to 0.
```

steps.title: Confirm No request or usage data found during CPU… is resolved
steps.description:
Verify the fix by checking that the original error no longer occurs:
```bash
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=opencost -n opencost --tail=50 --since=5m
kubectl get events -n opencost --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm that the issue symptoms are gone.

resolution.summary: Hey ! Thanks for the details provided here. Those logs can be safely ignored at this time! You can reduce the log level by setting the `LOG_LEVEL=warn` environment variable.

USER-SUPPLIED VALUES:
extraVolumes:
- configMap:
    name: opencost-aws
  name: custom-configs
nameOverride: opencost
opencost:
  customPricing:
    configPath: /tmp/custom-config
    configmapName: custom-pricing-model
    createConfigmap: true
    enabled: false
  exporter:
    defaultClusterId: iob-eks-1
    extraEnv:
      AWS_PRICING_URL: https://pricing.us-east-1.amazonaws.com/offers/v1.0/aws/AmazonEC2/current/eu-west-1/index.json
      CONFIG_PATH: /tmp/custom-config
    extraVolumeMounts:
    - mountPath: /tmp/custom-config
      name: custom-configs
    replicas: 2
    resources:
      limits:
        cpu: 1000m
        memory: 1Gi
      requests:
        cpu: 10m
        memory: 55Mi
  metrics:
    serviceMonitor:
      additionalLabels:
        lnrs.io/monitoring-platform: "true

2023-11-21T08:19:01.402917562Z WRN No request or usage data found during CPU allocation calculation. Setting allocation to 0.

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with opencost installed or the issue environment reproducible.
